commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Tatoosh Islands Wilderness Kayak Adventure
commerceProducts.description: Join our experienced kayak guide for a thrilling sea kayak paddle adventure in Tongass National Forest. Your 4.5-hour sea kayak journey begins with a motorized boat ride to Tatoosh Islands. On a protected beach, you’ll learn about kayak safety. Then, launch from the beach in a stable, double sea kayak. Paddle among the islands and learn about the natural wonders of a temperate rainforest environment. Keep on the lookout for intertidal life, including bald eagles, seals, minks, deer, porpoise, sea lions, and whales.
highlights: Launch in your double see kayak to see a variety of plant and animal life in the Tongass National Forest.
Float over to the Tatoosh Islands on a scenic inflatable boat ride while learning about kayak safety.

commerceProducts.title: 5-Glacier Seaplane Exploration
commerceProducts.description: Get ready to experience Juneau like you never imagined - high above it! Your epic glacier adventure begins the moment you board your seaplane. Feel your heart race as you take off and soar over the Tongass National Forest, your segway into a birds eye view of five unique glaciers. The ,500 square mile Juneau Icefield will leave you breathless as you explore deep mountain crevasses, crystal-blue meltwater pools and the mystic icecaps of these majestic wonders.
highlights: Soar high above the Tongass National Forest.
Explore Juneau's most majestic glaciers.
Guests enjoy a window seat and a headset for continuous narration.

source.altText: Yukon suspension bridge.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Railroad and Yukon Suspension Bridge Discovery

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 80-100% booked

commerceProducts.description: Delight in Skagway's fascinating history and spectacular mountainous scenery as you travel to the White Pass Summit and the Yukon Suspension Bridge. Ride a restored vintage rail car on the White Pass & Yukon Route Railway, "The Scenic Railway of the World", as you delight to tales of the Klondike Gold Rush. Next, arrive at Fraser, British Columbia, and ride in the comfort of a motor coach across the White Pass summit to Tutshi Canyon where you can traverse the Yukon Suspension Bridge and enjoy the breathtaking view.
highlights: Admire breathtaking wilderness scenery while on an unforgettable journey on the White Pass & Yukon Route Railway, a narrow-gauge railway chiseled into a mountain during the Gold Rush.
Feel the adrenaline rush of crossing the Yukon Suspension Bridge, dangling 57 feet above a river roiling with class III and IV rapids.
Delve into the fascinating history of the region relayed by your informative guide, and stop for photos along the way at places such as Tormented Valley and Pitchfork Falls.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show and Crab Feast

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 60-79% booked

commerceProducts.description: From the high-energy competition of the Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show to the fresh flavors of the Dungeness crab feast, this dynamic shore excursion captures the essence of Ketchikan, Alaska's "First City."
highlights: Venture into the Thomas Basin historic district, Ketchikan’s charming downtown. Pass by Spruce Mill – once the largest Sitka Spruce Mill in the world and now home to the Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show arena.

Prepare for a thrilling display of Alaskan strength and skill, as you take your seat for the show, where world champion lumberjacks compete in 12 action-packed events, including axe throwing, log rolling, chopping, sawing, and tree climbing.
After the show, make your way to the Harbor Haus Pavilion for an unforgettable Dungeness crab feast, showcasing Alaska’s finest seafood.
As you dine, enjoy stunning views of the Thomas Basin Harbor, home to the Salmon Fishing Fleet and Crab Fishing Boats.
Conclude your Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show and Crab Feast excursion in the heart of downtown Ketchikan, where you can take the time to explore on your own before heading back to port.

source.altText: lush gardens in Victoria Canada.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Enchanting Butchart Gardens

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 60-79% booked

commerceProducts.description:
Explore Victoria, renowned as Canada’s “City of Gardens” on this scenic drive through the historic downtown and visit to one of the world’s most recognized gardens—the breathtaking Butchart Gardens. Victoria’s gardens bloom year-round. Enjoy a scenic drive through the historic downtown quarter, viewing Victoria’s various districts as well as the Parliament Buildings and the iconic Empress Hotel. Then, travel to the enchanting Butchart Gardens, known as one of the best show gardens in the world. Stroll the Sunken Garden, the Rose Garden, the Japanese Garden, Star Pond, the Italian Garden, and the Mediterranean Garden. Learn about the garden’s diverse plant species at the Plant Identification Centre. Children can enjoy the Children’s Pavilion with its carousel. Butchart Gardens is a family-owned and operated National Historic Site, featuring over a million budding plants that bloom from March through October. Butchart Gardens has been celebrated by National Geographic, CNN, and Condé Nast.  
This tour is provided by an operator that has been third-party certified to meet the Global Sustainable Tourism Council's Criteria for Tour Operators.
highlights: Learn how the Butchart family transformed a barren rock quarry into one of the world's top horticulture attractions.
Enjoy a self-guided tour through this captivating and colorful botanical wonderland spanning 55 acres.
Take a drive through Chinatown and Victoria's historic Inner Harbor.

commerceProducts.title: Destintion Highlight: Bear Country and Wildlife Expedition

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 80-100% booked

commerceProducts.description: Embark on a scenic nature and cultural adventure in Ketchikan with your own personal naturalist. Your day begins with a visit to Herring Cove, where you'll search for Black Bears in their natural habitat. You'll also have an opportunity to view runs of Pacific Salmon and an array of other wildlife. Enhanced trails through the dense rainforest will lead you on a picturesque walk, enjoying stops at select elevated locations for prime bear viewing. the Carving Center, where a master totem carver crafts totems from local red cedar logs. Finally visit our totem park featuring 17 standing Tsimshian Poles.
highlights: View the abundance of wildlife as you walk through the heart of the dense rainforest.
Observe salmon runs in Eagle Creek and estuary.
Enjoy natural surroundings in Ketchikan in search of Black Bears in their natural habitat in Herring Cove.

gold on grungy wash pan with river sand.
family admiring gold at the big nugget in Alaska.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Big Nugget
commerceProducts.description: Enter the Gold Rush Training Camp, to learn how to extract paydirt, sluice, and assay your results. Learn the art of gold panning with a humorous and educational show. Practice your technique in heated troughs. Authentic gold is guaranteed! Weigh your fortune with our assayers. Meet canine athletes that have run the world-famous Iditarod race as well as our experienced mushers. Hear about the history of this breed through personal stories and an amazing collection of the gear necessary on long-distance races like the Iditarod. Observe the musher hooking them up to an authentic sled for a fast practice lap around the track. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed! Take a guided tour of the 350-ton Sixty Mile Dredge – one of the last rescued authentic gold dredges used for placer mining. Take a break with lunch at the Alaskan BBQ where our menu is based on Alaskan home-grown foods and finish with a campfire complete with s'mores, or soda floats (weather dependent), lots of performances, and laughter with our costumed characters. You will return to the ship not as a cheechako (newcomer to Alaska) but as a seasoned sourdough (someone who has experienced Alaska and is now a part of its history and culture) with lots of memories to share back home.
highlights: Meet Iditarod dogs and their mushers, and learn about the role sled dogs played in Alaska’s history. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed!
Visit the gold dredge and uncover its inner workings.
Savor a BBQ buffet, enjoying smoked ribs, chicken, salmon chowder delicious sides and dessert on the riverside patios.
Enjoy a campfire complete with s'mores or soda floats (weather dependent), lots of performances, and laughter with our costumed characters.
Become a prospector of the Gold Rush era and learn various gold panning techniques and search for authentic Yukon gold at the warm-water troughs, where valuable gold is guaranteed!

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: City Tour of Scenic Victoria
commerceProducts.description: Embark on a leisurely sightseeing drive that provides a captivating look at Victoria, while you relax in complete comfort. Your narrated exploration begins with a drive through extraordinary residential neighborhoods, where grand estates with manicured gardens overlook the Strait of San Juan de Fuca and the soaring Olympic Mountains. You'll witness Beacon Hill, the city's sprawling park located on the shores of the strait. The tour concludes with a drive along the Inner Harbor, the very heartbeat of Victoria. Here, you'll have the chance to debark at the historic Fairmont Empress Hotel and explore the Inner Harbor independently. Or, if you prefer, continue back to the ship. This tour is provided by an operator that has been third-party certified to meet the Global Sustainable Tourism Council's Criteria for Tour Operators.
highlights: Discover the beauty and history of Victoria, known as one of the most picturesque cities in North America.
Visit the Inner Harbor, the lively and historic centerpiece of the city.
See the Legislative Building, the Empress Hotel, the Royal British Columbia Museum and other sites.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Gold Fever and Alaskan Sled Dogs
commerceProducts.description: Your adventure begins when you disembark and meet your costumed guide who “lives” in 1898 and is there to accompany you on your Alaskan experience. Your tour includes an amazing encounter with sled dogs and mushers, and a heaping dose of gold fever. Your ticket includes everything you need for a trip to the Klondike. Enter the Gold Rush Training Camp, to learn how to extract paydirt, sluice, and assay your results. Learn the art of gold panning with a humorous and educational show. Practice your technique in heated water troughs. Authentic gold is guaranteed! Weigh your fortune with our assayers. Meet canine athletes that have run the world-famous Iditarod race as well as experienced mushers. Hear about the history of this breed through personal stories and an amazing collection of all the gear necessary on long-distance races like the Iditarod. Observe the musher hooking them up to an authentic sled for a fast practice lap around the track. The next generation of puppies who are there for you to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed! Return to the ship not as a cheechako (newcomer to Alaska) but as a seasoned sourdough (someone who has experienced Alaska and is now a part of its history and culture) with lots of memories to share back home.
highlights: Meet Iditarod dogs and their mushers, and learn about the role sled dogs played in Alaska’s history. The next generation of puppies are there to cuddle and hold. Puppy kisses guaranteed!
Become a prospector of the Gold Rush era and learn various gold panning techniques and search for authentic Yukon gold at the warm-water troughs, where valuable gold is guaranteed!

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Canadian Ale Trail and City Highlights
commerceProducts.description: Explore the unofficial craft brew capital of Canada on foot as you bend an elbow at three popular breweries with your guides to sample frothy, locally produced beers, each with distinct flavor profiles and histories.
highlights: Depart by bus from the cruise terminal on your narrated tour along the water and past the beautiful Beacon Hill park to the center of downtown Victoria, where you will set out on foot with your local guide and visit three local breweries: Whistle Buoy Brewing Company, Herald Street Brew Works, and Swans Brewery.

At your first stop, Whistle Buoy Brewing Company, in the 19th century mall known as Market Square, sample one of its hoppy IPAs, rich stouts, or crisp lagers, all crafted with care using fresh, local ingredients.

Continue foot through historic Chinatown and Fan Tan Alley, to Herald Street Brew Works, where fine beer standards are complemented by creative recipes, including its popular coffee-infused beer made with locally roasted beans.

Finish by raising a glass at Swans Brewery, where you will enjoy a frothy mug from a brewery known throughout the region for its classic lagers and ales, as well as creative ciders and sours, and satisfy your appetite with an appetizer from its tempting menu.

Afterwards, re-board your bus and continue with a narrated drive through downtown Victoria and the picturesque Inner Harbor, highlighting the Legislative Buildings and the iconic Château-style Fairmont Empress Hotel, before returning to the port.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Secret Garden, City Highlights and High Tea

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 60-79% booked

commerceProducts.description: Embrace the natural beauty and royal legacy of Victoria on a journey to one of the top 100 small gardens in the world, complete with traditional high tea, before taking in the city sights and the bustling downtown.
highlights: Arrive to the Abkhazi Garden, created by Georgian Prince and Princess Abkhazi, for exclusive early entrance into this hidden gem teeming with leafy pathways lined with rocky slopes and turtle-filled ponds, in 1946.
Take a serene stroll past Garry oaks, Japanese maples, aromatic roses, 50-year-old rhododendrons, and flowers of every color.
Continue to The Teahouse for Royal Abkhazi high tea and a selection of Silk Road loose leaf teas in a tranquil setting overlooking the gardens.
Delight in a three-tier assortment of scones, quiche, and sandwiches that showcase regional ingredients and produce grown in the surrounding garden.
Afterwards, enjoy a sightseeing drive through the heart of downtown Victoria, where you can explore the city's British Colonial past, souvenir shops, or seafood restaurants on your own.

source.altText: flowers at Glacier Garden.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Glacier Gardens and Alaska State Museum

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 60-79% booked

commerceProducts.description: Your adventure begins with a fully narrated tour of downtown Juneau, Alaska's capital, on the way to Glacier Gardens, 50 acres of rainforest near the Tongass National Park. "The Tongass" comprises the world's last surviving temperate rainforest and the largest national park in the U.S. There, join a knowledgeable forest guide aboard a covered shuttle for the mile-long journey into the heart of the rainforest, which affords breathtaking views of the Mendenhall Valley, carved out by the glacier. Then, expand your understanding with a visit to the Alaska State Museum, which reveals artifacts from the state's rich history, including its Native roots and its time as a Russian territory.
highlights: Explore the Alaska State Museum, which showcases the diverse cultures that have inhabited this rustically beautiful land.
Ascend to a 580-foot elevation and gaze in awe at spectacular views of the Mendenhall Valley, Chilkat Mountains, and Gastineau Channel.
